content.full_text:
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 ••• 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814 
(916) 322·5662 
Marc Hynes 
Administration 
322·5660 
499 So. Sunnyvale Road 
Sunnyvale, CA 
94086 
Dear Mr. Hynes: 
Executive/Legal 
321·5901 
Enforcement 
322·6441 
November 2, 1983 
Statements of Economic Interest 
322·6444 
Re: 
Advice Letter No. A-83-246 
During a recent telephone conversation, we discussed the 
disclosure and disqualification provisions of the Political 
Reform Act. 
This letter is to confirm our conversation.l/ 
In discussing the disclosure provisions, we focused on the 
disclosure of clients' names. 
An official who owns less than 
10 percent of a business entity is not required to disclose 
clients' names.£/ 
Government Code Section 82030.1/ 
If the 
official owns a 10 percent or greater interest in the business 
entity, he or she must disclose a client's name if the 
official's pro rata share of the client's fees to the entity 
during the year amount to $10,000 or more. 
Sections 82030 and 
87207(b) (3). 
Thus, if an official owns 50 percent of a law 
firm, and the firm received $20,000 in fees from a client, his 
or her pro rata share of the fees is $10,000 and the client's 
name must be disclosed on Schedule H of the official's Statement 
of Economic Interests. 
1/ Our conversation was prompted by an article in the 
Gilroy Dispatch (October 4, 1983) entitled "New Conflict of 
Interest Law Questioned by 2 Councilmen" in which the disclosure 
and disqualification provisions were misstated. 
£/ 
He or she must disclose the business entity as a 
source of income of $250 or more. 
1/ Hereinafter all statutory references are to the 
Government Code unless otherwise indicated. 


Marc Hynes 
November 2, 1983 
Page 2 
Commission regulation, 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18740 (copy 
enclosed) provides that an official may be exempted from 
_. 
disclosing a client's name if the disclosure of the name will 
violate a legally recognized privilege under California law. 
Under current law, a name is protected under the attorney-client 
privilege only when facts concerning an attorney's 
representation of an anonymous client are publicly known and 
those facts, when coupled with disclosure of the client's 
identity, might expose the client to an official investigation 
or to criminal or civil liability. 
(See Hays v. Wood (1979) 25 
Cal. 3d 772, 785.) 
The disqualification provisions of the Act require an 
official to abstain from making or participating in the making 
of a decision if the official knows or has reason to know that 
he or she has a -financial interest. (Section 87100.) 
An 
official has a financial interest in a decision within the 
meaning of Section 87100 if it is reasonably foreseeable that 
the decision will have a material financial effect, distinguish-
able from its effect on the public generally,il on: 
* * * 
(c) 
Any source of income ••• aggregating $250 or 
more in value provided to, received by, or 
promised to the public official within 12 months 
prior to the time when the decision is made •••• 
Section 87103(c). 
In the case of an official who owns a 10 percent or greater 
interest in a business entity, the official's sources of income 
are the entity and any clients who, within the 12 months, 
provided the entity with sufficient fees so that the official's 
pro rata share is $250 or more. 
The official must disqualify 
himself or herself from decisions affecting these sources of 
income when it is appropriate under Sections 87100 and 
87103,~1 whether or not the official is required to disclose 
the names of those clients under Section 87207 (b) (3) • 
il See the enclosed copy of 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 
18703 which discusses the "public generally" exception. 
~I See the enclosed copy of 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 
18702 which defines the phrase "material financial effect." 


Marc Hynes 
November 2, 1983 
Page 3 
I hope I have clarified the disclosure and disqualification 
provisions for you. 
If I can be of any additional help, please 
feel free to contact me at (916) 322-5901. 
Please provide- -each 
of your councilmembers with one of the enclosed copies of this 
letter. 
JSM:plh 
Enclosure 
Very truly yours, 
1'"'1 
? 
_- '-
-J c_ g-
:'--"../ 
Janis Shank McLean 
Counsel 
Legal Division

sections.question_synthetic: What are the disclosure requirements for client names under the Political Reform Act, and when must an official disqualify themselves from decisions due to financial interests in business entities?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Officials owning less than 10% of a business entity need not disclose client names, but those owning 10% or more must disclose if their pro rata share of client fees reaches $10,000 annually. Disqualification is required when decisions could materially affect income sources of $250 or more, including clients whose fees generate that threshold amount.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document structured as confirmation letter rather than Q&A format; legal analysis embedded in narrative text. References enclosed regulation copies (2 Cal. Adm. Code Sections 18740, 18703, 18702). Footnotes provide additional context. Clear conclusion synthesizable from substantive analysis provided.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: What are the disclosure requirements for client names under the Political Reform Act, and when must an official disqualify themselves from decisions due to financial interests in business entities?

CONCLUSION: Officials owning less than 10% of a business entity need not disclose client names, but those owning 10% or more must disclose if their pro rata share of client fees reaches $10,000 annually. Disqualification is required when decisions could materially affect income sources of $250 or more, including clients whose fees generate that threshold amount.

embedding.summary: FPPC advice letter confirming disclosure and disqualification requirements under the Political Reform Act regarding client names and financial interests in business entities, responding to questions prompted by media coverage of conflict of interest law.
